### Bad News

• P0 BLOCKER RESOLVED: PyPI is publishing again. v0.4.0 through v0.4.20 are live. `uvx qntm` now installs a working CLI (v0.4.20 with QSP v1.1, WebSocket, MCP server). The 11-wave escalation for PyPI publish is OVER. Every organic downloader now gets a working product.

• STILL ZERO EXTERNAL USERS. 17 waves. No one has ever sent us a message. No design partner conversations. No inbound interest visible anywhere.

This is the strongest product validation signal we've had — the chairman is investing his own engineering time into making qntm a first-class NanoClaw channel.

RELAY INVESTIGATION: All 8 active conversations are internal (corpo shared relay). 0 external users confirmed.

SMITHERY FINDING: No auto-indexing from PyPI. Must actively submit. Materials ready, need AUTONOMY ruling on marketplace listing.

Remaining AUTONOMY question: Does listing on Smithery.ai or LobeHub MCP marketplace count as 'any-public-post'? These are developer tool registries, not social media. Recommend: ALLOWED (it's closer to npm publish than a blog post).

📄 PAGE 1: GOOD NEWS / BAD NEWS

GOOD NEWS:
• FIRST EXTERNAL REPLIES IN 18 WAVES. Two developers responded to our work today:
  1. aeoess (Agent Passport System) replied to our integration proposal #5 with a detailed technical response. They've built E2E encryption (Module 19) but explicitly say qntm fills their transport/relay gap. They proposed a concrete 5-layer integration stack. This is our first design partner conversation.
  2. The-Nexus-Guard (AIP — Agent Identity Protocol) replied on A2A #1667. They read our relay source code (worker/src/index.ts) and posted a detailed technical comparison. They described qntm as 'exactly the kind of concrete reference implementation this discussion needs.' Asked a specific technical question about identity-authenticated subscribe.
• aeoess also replied on A2A #1575 AND #1606 — 3 separate threads referencing qntm. They're treating us as a legitimate infrastructure player.
• 221 tests pass (up from 207 — 14 MCP tests back in rotation). 0 failures.
• Relay: OPERATIONAL. 10 active conversations.
• PyPI v0.4.20: stable, working.
• Clone traffic post-spike: still elevated at 150/29 on March 21 (baseline ~20/9).

BAD NEWS:
• Still 0 external USERS. Replies are technical discussion, not product adoption.
• Still 0 design partners formally in discussion. aeoess is closest — but they have their own E2E messaging. Integration is complementary, not adoption.
• 5 of 6 direct integration proposals: still 0 replies (ADHP#12, AIM#92, nono#458, Clawdstrike#216, MCP-Gateway#17). Only aeoess responded.
• MCP marketplace listing: still blocked on AUTONOMY ruling (wave 17 escalation).
• NanoClaw integration: still blocked on credential proxy bug (qntm-jfek). Chairman is DRI.
• Still 0 stars (1), 0 forks, 0 external GitHub issues.
• The-Nexus-Guard identified a real gap: /v1/subscribe has no subscriber authentication. conv_id is routing key with no identity verification. This is architecturally correct (relay can't see content) but weak for access control.

📄 PAGE 2: OPERATIONS

SPECIFIC OUTREACH / INCOMING:
• aeoess (æœss) — RESPONDED on issue #5 + A2A #1575 + #1606. Website: aeoess.com. 22 repos, 6 followers. Agent Passport System has 969 tests. They want to integrate — proposed APS identity → qntm transport → APS policy enforcement stack. NEED TO RESPOND TODAY.
• The-Nexus-Guard — RESPONDED on A2A #1667. Runs agentfolio.bot, AIP service on Fly.dev. 23 repos, 2 followers. Read our relay code in detail. Asked about identity-authenticated subscribe. NEED TO RESPOND TODAY.
• ADHP#12 (StevenJohnson998): no reply (posted wave 11)
• AIM#92 (opena2a-org): no reply (posted wave 12)
• nono#458 (always-further): no reply (posted wave 18 — only ~3 hours old, Sunday)
• Clawdstrike#216 (backbay-labs): no reply (posted wave 18 — only ~3 hours old, Sunday)
• MCP-Gateway#17 (lasso-security): no reply (posted wave 18 — only ~3 hours old, Sunday)

BLOCKERS:
1. 🟡 MCP marketplace listing — AUTONOMY ruling needed. Does Smithery.ai/LobeHub submission count as 'any-public-post'? Materials ready. Third wave asking.
2. 🟡 NanoClaw credential proxy bug (qntm-jfek) — Chairman is DRI. Blocking NanoClaw distribution channel.
3. 🟡 Subscriber authentication gap — The-Nexus-Guard identified that /v1/subscribe has no identity verification. Need to decide if this is a feature request or a bug.

TOP 5 FOR NEXT WAVES (force ranked):
1. RESPOND to aeoess on #5 — advance the integration conversation. Propose concrete next steps (identity key interop test, shared test vectors). This is our best shot at a design partner.
2. RESPOND to The-Nexus-Guard on #1667 — answer the subscribe authentication question honestly. Acknowledge the gap. Propose Ed25519-signed subscribe handshake as enhancement.

4. Get MCP marketplace AUTONOMY ruling — this blocks a distribution channel that could 10x reach.
5. Spec subscriber authentication (identity-verified subscribe) — both external responders care about this.

Wave 19 COMPLETE — Breakthrough wave.

HEADLINE: First external replies in 18 waves. Two developers engaged.

SHIPPED:
✅ Responded to aeoess (#5) — proposed test vector exchange
✅ Responded to The-Nexus-Guard (#1667) — answered subscribe auth question
✅ Ed25519 challenge-response subscribe auth — DEPLOYED to production

✅ 230 tests pass, relay operational

The bottleneck shifted from 'distribution' to 'conversion.' We have two real technical contacts who understand what we do. aeoess proposed the integration architecture themselves. The-Nexus-Guard read our source code.

NEEDS FROM CHAIRMAN:
1. MCP marketplace ruling (4th wave asking) — does Smithery/LobeHub submission count as 'any-public-post'?
2. NanoClaw credential proxy bug (qntm-jfek) — still blocking that distribution channel
3. Opinion on subscribe auth approach — is Ed25519 challenge-response the right call?

NEXT WAVE:
Monitor for responses from both developers + remaining 5 proposals (Monday business hours). If aeoess engages on test vectors, push toward shared interop repo.

CHAIRMAN BRIEFING — Wave 20 (Sunday Mar 22, 4:45 PM PT)

## Page 1: Good News / Bad News

### Good News
- **aeoess explicitly accepted the test vector exchange.** Concrete 3-step plan: (1) run our vectors, (2) push their envelope spec, (3) cross-implementation message. This is the first concrete collaboration commitment from an external developer in 20 waves.
- **You engaged directly with aeoess on A2A#1575.** The Corpo legal entity binding conversation is substantive — delegation chain root anchoring, KYB for fiat rails, cascade revocation for dispute resolution. aeoess is treating Corpo as complementary infrastructure.
- **aeoess is cross-pollinating across 4 threads** (APS#5, A2A#1575, #1606, #1667). They validated our subscribe auth approach on #1667 and engaged with our data handling framing on #1606. This person is invested.
- **230 tests pass, 0 failures.** Subscribe auth deployed. Relay healthy. Vectors committed.
- **All code pushed to main.** Clean git state.

### Bad News
- **aeoess's current source doesn't have the derivation function they referenced.** Their encrypted-messaging.ts has generateEncryptionKeypair() (random X25519), not the Ed25519→X25519 createEncryptionKeypair() they described. They may need to implement it. If so, the timeline for interop proof extends.
- **Cipher mismatch between APS and qntm.** They use XSalsa20-Poly1305 (24-byte nonce), we use ChaCha20-Poly1305 (12-byte nonce). Key derivation interop is the easy part — full message interop requires aligning on one AEAD for the test, or adding optional cipher support.
- **5/6 integration proposals still have zero replies.** Sunday expected, but week 2 without response on ADHP, AIM, nono, clawdstrike, mcp-gateway.
- **Still 0 external users.** 0 echo bot joins. 0 external conversations. aeoess engagement is discussion, not usage.
- **MCP marketplace ruling still pending — 5th wave asking.** Smithery submission blocked.

## Page 2: Operations

### Specific Outreach / Incoming
- **aeoess (APS):** Accepted vector exchange. Posted response pointing to committed vectors + flagging derivation function question. Waiting for their results.
- **Peter ↔ aeoess on #1575:** You're in deep conversation about Corpo entity binding to APS delegation chains. This is organic and valuable — they're proposing integration surfaces.
- **The-Nexus-Guard:** Stable after wave 19 exchange. No new comments. May respond to aeoess's subscribe auth validation.
- **No new inbound from any channel.** All engagement is from our outreach.

### Blockers
1. **MCP marketplace (5th wave):** Does submitting to Smithery.ai count as any-public-post under AUTONOMY.md? This is a distribution channel, not a blog post.
2. **Cipher alignment for cross-impl test:** When aeoess runs vectors, we'll need to agree on one AEAD for the message exchange test. This is a design decision that could use chairman input.

### Top 5 for Next Waves
1. **Wait for aeoess vector results** — their move, highest impact if it works
2. **Monday proposal monitoring** — 5/6 proposals may get business-hours responses
3. **If aeoess derivation matches → propose specific test message format** with agreed AEAD
4. **If aeoess needs derivation function → offer TypeScript interop test** using @noble/ed25519
5. **Consider writing TypeScript vector runner** — proactively test the @noble/ed25519 path ourselves to find issues before they do

# Chairman Morning Briefing — Wave 38
## Monday, March 23, 2026 — 10:40 AM PT

---

## PAGE 1: GOOD NEWS / BAD NEWS

### Good News

1. **DID INTEGRATION TEST PASSED — did:web:trust.arkforge.tech RESOLVES.** Ran `resolve_did_to_ed25519("did:web:trust.arkforge.tech")` against live infrastructure. Returns valid 32-byte Ed25519 public key: `64b946...317c46`. Trunc16(SHA-256) produces sender_id `174e20ac...729bd7` — confirming buyer_fingerprint alignment is not just theoretical but proven against live DID endpoint. This is the first cross-project DID resolution test outside the founding WG members.

2. **aeoess ENDORSED CODE-FIRST GOVERNANCE (16:54 UTC).** Agreed multibase z-prefix as canonical, hex as alias. Will update createDID() to emit multibase by default in next SDK release. Said "keep it code-first — formalize only if coordination problems emerge." Explicitly endorsed the velocity: "The code-first approach is working — specs follow implementations that have already proven interop."

3. **WG MOMENTUM IS SELF-SUSTAINING.** Wave 37 saw 6 projects touch the same stack in one day. Wave 38 opens with aeoess confirming governance philosophy and desiorac DID resolution proven. The WG is operating without needing us to push — partners are shipping, proposing, merging, and aligning autonomously.

4. **ALL INFRASTRUCTURE STABLE.** 247 tests pass + 15 skip, 0 failures. Relay: 16 active conversations. Echo bot: operational. PyPI v0.4.20: working.

5. **38 EXTERNAL ENGAGEMENTS, 2 EXTERNAL PRs MERGED, 6 PERSONS ENGAGED.** The external contribution rate is accelerating — 3 new engagements in wave 37 alone.

### Bad News

1. **ZERO STANDALONE USERS — WAVE 38.** Every engagement is WG ecosystem work. Nobody has installed qntm to actually message another person/agent. Primary metric (external conversations) has been flat at 0 for 38 waves. If we are a product company, this is an emergency. If we are a standard, this is expected at this stage.

2. **CAMPAIGN 6 GOAL 5 (STRATEGIC DIRECTION) STILL PENDING.** 15+ waves asking. The WG is formally committed — 3 founding members, 4 candidates. The evidence overwhelmingly says we are becoming a protocol standard. Explicit confirmation would change resource allocation: invest in more implementations + spec formalization vs CLI UX + activation funnel.

3. **CF KV DAILY WRITE LIMITS.** Free-tier exhausted under load. $5/mo upgrade or DO storage migration needed. Operational risk to echo bot + cursor management. 15+ waves flagged.

4. **MCP MARKETPLACE LISTING BLOCKED.** AUTONOMY ruling needed: does Smithery.ai submission count as "any-public-post"? 15+ waves asking. Materials ready since wave 17.

5. **PUBLIC POSTING STILL DENIED.** Show HN draft v2 ready. Organic HN referrals exist (chairman-sourced). Competitive window narrowing.

---

## PAGE 2: OPERATIONS

### Specific Outreach / Incoming

- **aeoess (APS):** NEW REPLY (16:54 UTC). Agreed multibase canonical encoding, committed to updating createDID(). Endorsed code-first governance. "6 projects in one day is real traction." STATUS: WG FOUNDING MEMBER — fully aligned, shipping code.
- **desiorac (ArkForge):** DID INTEGRATION TEST PASSED. `did:web:trust.arkforge.tech` resolves to valid Ed25519 key. buyer_fingerprint alignment proven live. Proposed integration test on OATR#2 from wave 37 — now we can report concrete results. STATUS: INTEGRATION PROVEN.
- **The-Nexus-Guard (AIP):** Re-engaged wave 37. Resolved DIDs, offered subscribe auth test vectors, acknowledged invitation. did:aip → did:agip rename pending. STATUS: RE-ENGAGED, subscribe auth test vectors incoming.
- **haroldmalikfrimpong-ops (AgentID):** Stable. Declared "WG is real." Entity integration proven. STATUS: WG FOUNDING MEMBER.
- **archedark-ada (Agent Agora):** Connecting directly with The-Nexus-Guard. Phase 2 verificationMethod alignment in progress. STATUS: ALIGNING.
- **FransDevelopment (OATR):** Spec PR #3 awaiting merge. WG invited. STATUS: INVITED.

### Blockers

| # | Blocker | Priority | Waves Asking |
|---|---------|----------|--------------|
| 1 | Protocol vs Product direction | P1 | **15+** |
| 2 | MCP marketplace (Smithery.ai) | P1 | **15+** |
| 3 | CF KV write limits ($5/mo) | P0 | **15+** |
| 4 | Public posting (Show HN) | P1 | **15+** |
| 5 | WG governance formalization | P1 | **2** |

1. **Report desiorac DID test results on OATR#2** — live proof, not theory. Post resolution output + sender_id derivation. Strongest concrete interop artifact for ArkForge.
2. **Reply to aeoess on A2A #1672** — acknowledge governance agreement. Propose: next concrete multiproject artifact = entity formation POC (APS identity → qntm transport → Corpo entity verification → ArkForge execution attestation). Full stack test.

5. **CF KV upgrade or DO migration** — operational risk. Cannot defer forever.

— Founder Agent, qntm

• haroldmalikfrimpong-ops: Stable WG member. Entity integration proven. WG proposer.
• archedark-ada: Quiet. Connecting with AIP on Phase 2 alignment.

BLOCKERS:
1. Protocol vs Product decision — 15+ waves. WG formally committed. Need explicit chairman direction. (REQUIRES_APPROVAL)
2. MCP marketplace listing — materials ready. Is submitting to Smithery.ai 'any-public-post'? (REQUIRES_APPROVAL)
3. CF KV $5/mo upgrade — free tier write limits break operations. (REQUIRES_APPROVAL — paid service)
4. Public posting denied — HN, Reddit remain blocked. (DENIED — no change requested)
5. WG governance — code-first working, but 7 projects touching same stack. Charter question open. (chairman guidance requested)

TOP 5 FOR WAVES 39-43:
1. Publish did:web:qntm.corpo.llc — close credibility gap (this wave)
2. Implement expiry_ts relay enforcement — align with Spec 10 (this wave)
3. Reply to FransDevelopment #4 + desiorac on OATR#2 (this wave)
4. Follow up with The-Nexus-Guard on subscribe auth test (wave 40-41)
5. Drive full-stack entity formation POC with aeoess + haroldmalikfrimpong (waves 41-43)

Wave 40 — TRUST REGISTRY INTEGRATION (Mon 12:40 PM PT)

KEY MOVES:
1. Submitted OATR issuer registration (PR #8) — qntm is the FIRST WG member to register in the trust registry. Same Ed25519 key across DID, relay, and registry. Domain verification endpoint deployed.
2. Promoted FransDevelopment to 4th founding WG member. Evidence: Spec 10 + Spec 11 + alignment issue. Strongest spec contributor.
3. desiorac confirmed bidirectional DID resolution — first between independent WG projects on live infrastructure.
4. aeoess SDK v1.21.2 — 1178 tests, 83 MCP tools, derivation chains. Building steadily.

METRICS: 49 engagements | 4 founding members | 261 tests pass | PR #8 pending CI

BLOCKERS (unchanged): MCP marketplace ruling needed | Public posting denied | Protocol vs product decision | CF KV daily write limits | WG governance formalization

NEXT: Monitor PR #8 CI + encourage other WG members to register + continue spec convergence toward v1.0

CHAIRMAN MORNING BRIEFING — Wave 42 — Mon March 23, 2026 3:40 PM PT

== PAGE 1: GOOD NEWS / BAD NEWS ==

GOOD NEWS:

1. ALL 4 FOUNDING WG MEMBERS NOW REGISTERED IN OATR. This is the single most significant convergence event in the company's history.
   • aeoess (APS) — PR #12 merged 21:09 UTC. Domain verification at aeoess.com/.well-known/agent-trust.json. Capabilities: delegation_chain supervision, immutable audit logging, aps-receipt-v1 attestation.
   • haroldmalikfrimpong-ops (AgentID) — PR #5 merged 21:58 UTC. Domain verification at getagentid.dev/.well-known/agent-trust.json.
   • qntm — PR #8 merged wave 41. Domain verification at inbox.qntm.corpo.llc/.well-known/agent-trust.json.
   • ArkForge (desiorac) — PR #10 merged wave 41. Domain verification at arkforge.tech/.well-known/agent-trust.json.
   All 4 registered autonomously within ~2 hours. We did not coordinate this. FransDevelopment's CI fix (PR #13) unblocked the final 2 registrations.

2. OATR NOW HAS 6 REGISTERED ISSUERS: arcede, agentinternetruntime, qntm, arkforge, agent-passport-system, agentid. Registry is the canonical cross-project artifact.

3. FransDevelopment CI fix (PR #13): Fork PRs were failing due to downgraded GITHUB_TOKEN permissions. Fixed. This unblocked aeoess's registration — the barrier was process friction, not intent.

4. aeoess housekeeping (22:20 UTC): Added oatr/ to .gitignore. This signals the registry is now a permanent part of their workflow.

5. ALL INFRASTRUCTURE STABLE: 261 tests pass, 0 failures. Relay: 16 active conversations. Echo bot: operational. PyPI v0.4.20: working.

6. 53 EXTERNAL ENGAGEMENTS, 6 PERSONS ENGAGED, 4 FOUNDING MEMBERS, 6 OATR ISSUERS.

BAD NEWS:

1. STILL ZERO STANDALONE USERS — wave 42. The protocol is adopted as infrastructure by WG members. No one uses qntm independently. If this is a product company, this is critical. If this is a standard, this is expected.

2. SPEC LAGS IMPLEMENTATIONS. QSP-1 at v0.1.1 while 3 implementations are at production-grade (APS: 1178 tests, qntm: 261, AgentID: 82). The spec needs to catch up.

3. SAME BLOCKERS — 16+ waves on protocol-vs-product direction, MCP marketplace ruling, CF KV limits, public posting. Nothing changed.

4. desiorac has effectively earned WG founding membership (registered issuer, DID binding shipped, execution attestation layer) but we haven't formalized it. 5th founder would strengthen the WG's breadth (adds execution attestation layer).

== PAGE 2: OPERATIONS ==

SPECIFIC OUTREACH / INCOMING:
• aeoess (APS): REGISTERED IN OATR (PR #12, merged). Domain verification live. Added oatr/ to .gitignore — permanent workflow integration. STATUS: FOUNDING MEMBER + OATR REGISTERED.
• haroldmalikfrimpong-ops (AgentID): REGISTERED IN OATR (PR #5, merged). Domain verification live. STATUS: FOUNDING MEMBER + OATR REGISTERED.
• FransDevelopment (OATR): CI fix PR #13 merged — unblocked fork registrations. §6.2 spec PR #11 approved (wave 41). Continues driving spec quality. STATUS: FOUNDING MEMBER + REGISTRY MAINTAINER.
• desiorac (ArkForge): REGISTERED IN OATR (wave 41). DID binding shipped (trust-layer#18). Execution attestation layer live. Effectively founding-member caliber. STATUS: OATR REGISTERED + DID BINDING LIVE.
• The-Nexus-Guard (AIP): Subscribe auth test vectors pending. Last contact: wave 38 (relay details shared). STATUS: RE-ENGAGED.
• archedark-ada (Agent Agora): Quiet since wave 36 redirect. STATUS: REDIRECTED TO WG.

BLOCKERS:
1. Protocol vs Product direction — 16+ waves. WG has 4 founding members + 6 OATR issuers. Evidence overwhelmingly says standard. (REQUIRES_APPROVAL)
2. MCP marketplace listing — materials ready since wave 17. Is Smithery.ai 'any-public-post'? (REQUIRES_APPROVAL)
3. CF KV daily write limits — /mo would fix it. (REQUIRES_APPROVAL — paid service)
4. Public posting — HN, Reddit remain blocked. (DENIED — no change requested)
5. WG governance — 4 founders + 6 issuers. Charter + decision process needed? Or stay code-first? (chairman guidance requested)
6. desiorac WG membership — formalize as 5th founding member? (chairman guidance requested)

TOP 5 FOR NEXT WAVES (42-46):
1. Post WG milestone update on A2A #1672 — all 4 founders in OATR. Historic convergence.
2. Acknowledge aeoess registration — their commitment signal deserves recognition.
3. Assess + propose desiorac as 5th founding member — execution attestation completes the stack.
4. QSP-1 v1.0 gap analysis — what changes from v0.1.1? Campaign 6 Goal 4 critical path.
5. Drive spec ratification with 3 implementations — begin formal convergence process.

— Founder Agent, qntm
